name: AP Calculus AB
description: A college-level differential and integral calculus course following the AP Calculus AB curriculum (College Board Course and Exam Description). Covers the eight Big Ideas: limits and continuity, differentiation, contextual and analytical applications of differentiation, integration and accumulation of change, differential equations, and applications of integration. Aligned to the AP exam administered each May. Students leaving this course are prepared for first-semester college calculus or to sit the AP exam for credit.

modules.title: Limits & Continuity
modules.objectives: Estimate limits graphically and numerically; distinguish two-sided from one-sided limits; identify when a limit fails to exist; define continuity using the three-condition test; identify removable, jump, and infinite discontinuities.
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy AP Calc AB — Unit 1: Limits and Continuity
modules.practiceNote: AP Classroom — Unit 1 Personal Progress Check (FRQ + MCQ)
modules.assignment: Submit a write-up showing a limit estimated three ways for the same function: (1) from a graph, (2) from a numerical table approaching the value from both sides, (3) by direct substitution where valid. Write 2–3 sentences on why the three answers agree (or what kind of discontinuity makes them disagree).

modules.title: Limit Laws & the Squeeze Theorem
modules.titleZh: 极限运算法则与夹逼定理
modules.objectives: Apply the sum, difference, product, quotient, and power limit laws; evaluate limits algebraically using factoring and rationalization; recognize indeterminate forms; apply the Squeeze Theorem to compute limits like sin(x)/x as x→0.
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Limits via Algebraic Manipulation (15 problems)
modules.assignment: Evaluate eight given limits showing all algebraic steps. At least two must use the Squeeze Theorem and at least two must address an indeterminate 0/0 form by factoring or rationalization.

modules.title: Definition of the Derivative
modules.objectives: Define the derivative as the limit of the difference quotient; interpret f'(a) as the slope of the tangent line at x = a and as instantaneous rate of change; compute derivatives from the limit definition for polynomial functions.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Calculus Vol. 1 — §3.1 Defining the Derivative
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy — Derivative as Slope of Tangent Line
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Derivative as Limit of Difference Quotient (12 problems)
modules.assignment: For three given functions, compute f'(x) using the limit definition (no shortcuts). Then compute f'(2) and write the equation of the tangent line at x = 2.

modules.title: Basic Differentiation Rules
modules.objectives: Apply the power rule, constant multiple rule, sum/difference rule, product rule, and quotient rule; differentiate polynomial, rational, and root functions efficiently without the limit definition.
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy — Power, Product, and Quotient Rules
modules.video2Note: Professor Leonard — Product and Quotient Rule with Examples
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Differentiation Rules Mixed Practice (20 problems)
modules.assignment: Differentiate ten functions using the power, product, and quotient rules. Show one product-rule problem worked two ways (product rule vs. expanding first) and explain why both give the same answer.

modules.title: Chain Rule & Implicit Differentiation
modules.titleZh: 链式法则与隐函数求导
modules.objectives: Differentiate composite functions using the chain rule; identify the inner and outer functions correctly; use implicit differentiation when y is not isolated; find dy/dx for curves like x² + y² = 25.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Calculus Vol. 1 — §3.6 The Chain Rule and §3.8 Implicit Differentiation
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Chain Rule and Implicit Differentiation Mixed (15 problems)
modules.assignment: Solve six chain-rule problems and four implicit-differentiation problems. For one implicit problem, find the equation of the tangent line at a given point on the curve.

modules.title: Derivatives of Trig, Exponential, & Logarithmic Functions
modules.titleZh: 三角、指数与对数函数的导数
modules.objectives: Memorize derivatives of sin(x), cos(x), tan(x), e^x, and ln(x); apply chain rule with these standard derivatives; differentiate functions like e^(2x), ln(x²+1), and sin(3x).
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Calculus Vol. 1 — §3.5 Trigonometric Derivatives, §3.9 Derivatives of Exponentials and Logs
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy — Derivatives of Trig, Exp, Log
modules.video2Note: Professor Leonard — Derivatives of Exponential and Logarithmic Functions
modules.assignment: Differentiate twelve mixed expressions involving trig, exponential, and logarithmic functions. Include at least three that require the chain rule and at least one that combines all three families (e.g., e^(sin(ln x))).

modules.title: Related Rates
modules.objectives: Translate a real-world rate problem (a sliding ladder, a growing snowball, a draining tank) into an equation; differentiate both sides with respect to time; substitute given values only after differentiation; report units.
modules.video2Note: Professor Leonard — Related Rates: Ladder, Cone, and Shadow Problems
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Related Rates Mixed Practice (10 problems)
modules.assignment: Solve four related-rates problems with full setup: define variables, write the relating equation, differentiate, substitute, and report final units. At least one ladder/triangle problem and one cone/cylinder problem.

modules.title: Mean Value Theorem & Curve Sketching
modules.objectives: State and apply the Mean Value Theorem; use f' to identify intervals of increase/decrease and local extrema (first derivative test); use f'' for concavity and inflection points (second derivative test); sketch f(x) from sign charts of f' and f''.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Calculus Vol. 1 — §4.3 MVT, §4.5 Derivatives and Curve Shape
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy — Mean Value Theorem and First/Second Derivative Tests
modules.video2Note: Professor Leonard — First and Second Derivative Test
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — First and Second Derivative Test (15 problems)
modules.assignment: For two given functions: (1) find all critical points, (2) build a sign chart for f' and f'', (3) identify each local max/min and inflection point, (4) sketch the curve. Verify MVT applies on a specified interval and find the c value.

modules.title: Optimization Problems
modules.objectives: Read a word problem and translate the goal into a function of one variable using the constraint; identify the domain; find the absolute extremum on a closed interval or use the second derivative test; verify the answer makes physical sense.
modules.video2Note: Professor Leonard — Classic Box, Fence, and Distance Optimization Problems
modules.assignment: Solve three optimization problems: a box with maximum volume given fixed surface area, a fence enclosing maximum area given fixed perimeter on three sides, and a point on a curve closest to a given point. Show domain, derivative work, and sentence answer with units.

modules.title: Antiderivatives & Riemann Sums
modules.objectives: Compute antiderivatives of polynomial, exponential, and basic trig functions; understand Riemann sums (left, right, midpoint, trapezoidal) as approximations of accumulated change; recognize the definite integral as the limit of these sums.
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy — Riemann Sums and Antiderivatives
modules.assignment: For f(x) = x² on [0, 4] with n = 4 subintervals: compute the left, right, midpoint, and trapezoidal Riemann sums. Find the exact value using an antiderivative and explain which approximation came closest.

modules.title: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
modules.objectives: State both parts of the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus; evaluate definite integrals using antiderivatives (Part 2); differentiate accumulation functions like F(x) = ∫₁ˣ f(t)dt (Part 1); apply the chain rule when the upper limit is itself a function.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Calculus Vol. 1 — §5.3 The Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy — Fundamental Theorem of Calculus Parts 1 and 2
modules.video2Note: Professor Leonard — Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — FTC Parts 1 and 2 Mixed (15 problems)
modules.assignment: Evaluate six definite integrals using FTC Part 2. For an accumulation function F(x) = ∫₀ˣ f(t)dt (with f given as a piecewise graph), find F(2), F'(3), and identify where F is increasing.

modules.title: Techniques of Integration: u-Substitution
modules.objectives: Recognize when u-substitution applies (a function and a multiple of its derivative both appear); choose u correctly; transform the integral fully (including bounds for definite integrals); reverse-substitute when needed.
modules.assignment: Solve eight integrals using u-substitution. At least four must be definite integrals (transform the bounds — don't reverse-substitute). At least one must be a slightly hidden case where the constant has to be adjusted.

modules.title: Differential Equations & Slope Fields
modules.objectives: Recognize a differential equation; verify a proposed solution by substitution; sketch slope fields by hand for simple dy/dx = f(x,y); solve separable differential equations and apply initial conditions to find the particular solution.
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Calculus Vol. 2 — §4.1 Basics of Differential Equations, §4.3 Separable
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy — Slope Fields and Separable Equations
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Separable Equations with Initial Conditions (10 problems)
modules.assignment: Sketch the slope field for dy/dx = x − y at lattice points (–2,–2) to (2,2). Solve dy/dx = xy with y(0) = 2 by separation, then verify your particular solution by differentiation.

modules.title: Areas, Volumes & Cross-Sections
modules.objectives: Compute area between two curves using vertical or horizontal slices; compute volumes of revolution by the disk and washer methods around horizontal and vertical axes; compute volumes of solids with known cross-sections (squares, semicircles, equilateral triangles).
modules.readingNote: OpenStax Calculus Vol. 1 — Ch 6: Applications of Integration
modules.videoNote: Khan Academy — Areas, Disks, Washers, and Cross-Sections
modules.video2Note: Professor Leonard — Volumes of Revolution: Disks, Washers, Shells
modules.practiceNote: AP Classroom — Unit 8 Personal Progress Check (FRQ + MCQ)
modules.assignment: Solve three problems: (1) area between y = x² and y = x on [0,1]; (2) volume of revolution of y = √x on [0,4] about the x-axis using the disk method; (3) volume of a solid whose base is the region under y = x² on [0,2] with square cross-sections perpendicular to the x-axis.
